text:
CT CHEST, ABDOMEN, AND PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST
RESTAGING EXAMINATION

CLINICAL INDICATION:
Restaging evaluation in a 59-60-year-old female with a history of pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma of the head/uncinate process. Patient underwent pylorus-preserving pancreaticoduodenectomy (Whipple procedure) following neoadjuvant mFOLFIRINOX (8 cycles) and consolidative chemoradiation (IMRT 50.4 Gy with concurrent capecitabine). Final surgical pathology demonstrated ypT2 ypN2 M0 (4/24 lymph nodes positive, R0 resection). Pathologically confirmed hepatic metastases in segments VI and VII subsequently developed during adjuvant gemcitabine/capecitabine chemotherapy. Patient transitioned to second-line nanoliposomal irinotecan (nal-IRI, Onivyde) plus 5-FU/leucovorin (NAPOLI-1 regimen), now after 4 cycles. Evaluate for treatment response or disease progression.

COMPARISON:
Prior CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast obtained at transition to second-line therapy (prior to initiation of nal-IRI/5-FU/LV); prior MRI abdomen with hepatobiliary protocol (gadoxetate disodium); prior post-Whipple restaging CT.

TECHNIQUE:
Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed following administration of 100 mL of nonionic iodinated intravenous contrast material (isovuersol 370 mg/mL). Axial images acquired in the portal venous phase with coronal and sagittal reformats. Additional hepatic arterial phase images were obtained. Slice thickness 2.5 mm; scout topogram performed. Radiation dose: CTDIvol 12.4 mGy, DLP 520 mGy-cm.

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:
Lungs and Airways: The lungs are clear bilaterally. No focal consolidation, ground-glass opacity, or pleural effusion. No pulmonary nodules or masses identified. The airways are patent to the segmental level.
Mediastinum: The mediastinum is midline. No enlarged mediastinal or hilar lymph nodes by size criteria. No pericardial effusion. Cardiac silhouette is within normal limits.
Chest Wall and Pleura: No pleural thickening or pleural effusion. No osseous lesions within the thoracic spine, ribs, or visible clavicles to suggest metastatic involvement.

ABDOMEN - LIVER AND BILIARY SYSTEM:
The liver is normal in size and morphology. There are now four discrete hepatic lesions identified, all in the right hepatic lobe, representing interval change from prior imaging:

  - Segment VI lesion: Previously measured 0.9 cm at the time of second-line therapy initiation; the lesion now measures 2.1 cm in greatest axial dimension. The lesion demonstrates peripheral arterial hyperenhancement with central hypoenhancement on arterial phase, with washout of the peripheral enhancement relative to background liver parenchyma on portal venous phase. The internal architecture shows low-attenuation centrally, consistent with central necrosis within a rapidly enlarging deposit. Compared to the MRI hepatobiliary protocol examination performed several weeks prior to initiation of nal-IRI, this lesion has grown substantially.

  - Segment VII lesion: Previously measured 0.7 cm; now measures 1.6 cm in axial dimension. Enhancement pattern is similar to the segment VI lesion with peripheral arterial blush and portal venous washout. Margin is irregular and spiculated. No capsule identifiable.

  - Segment IV lesion (new): A new 1.2 cm hypodense lesion is identified in hepatic segment IVb, not present on the comparison CT or MRI. It demonstrates peripheral rim-like arterial enhancement with central non-enhancement and portal venous hypoattenuation relative to liver. Morphology and behavior are consistent with the other known metastatic deposits.

  - Segment VIII lesion (new): A new 0.8 cm hypodense nodule is present in segment VIII, likewise not identified on prior imaging. Enhancement characteristics are less conspicuous given its small size, though it demonstrates relative hypoattenuation to adjacent parenchyma on portal venous phase.

No additional lesions are identified in the left hepatic lobe. The hepatic parenchyma between lesions is homogeneous without evidence of steatosis or parenchymal infiltration. No biloma or perihepatic fluid collection. The surgical clips from the prior Whipple procedure are seen in the expected location of the hepaticojejunostomy, and there is no bile duct dilatation. The remnant pancreatic duct is not visualized, consistent with prior pancreaticoduodenectomy anatomy. The biliary stent placed during the initial ERCP is no longer in situ (consistent with exchange at time of Whipple).

ABDOMEN - SURGICAL BED AND PANCREAS:
The Whipple surgical bed is unchanged in appearance. Post-surgical fibrosis and scarring are present in the expected location of the pancreatic head and uncinate process. Surgical clips are distributed in the peripancreatic region, the superior mesenteric artery margin, and the hepatoduodenal ligament. There is no discrete soft tissue mass in the pancreatic bed. The pancreaticojejunostomy anastomosis is not separately visualized but there is no peripancreatic fluid collection, abscess, or anastomotic leak. The remnant pancreatic body and tail demonstrate post-pancreatectomy change with mild atrophy; no pancreatic duct dilatation in the remnant gland. No evidence of locoregional tumor recurrence in the surgical bed.

ABDOMEN - VASCULAR STRUCTURES:
The superior mesenteric artery and vein are patent. The portal vein and splenic vein are patent. No vascular thrombosis or encasement. No mesenteric or portal venous gas.

ABDOMEN - LYMPH NODES:
No enlarged mesenteric, retroperitoneal, or portal hepatis lymph nodes by size criteria. The previously noted peripancreatic lymph nodes are not discretely identified and have not enlarged. No new regional lymphadenopathy.

ABDOMEN - PERITONEUM AND ASCITES:
No ascites. No peritoneal implants or omental nodularity identified on this examination. Mesenteric fat is homogeneous without strand or nodule.

ABDOMEN - OTHER ABDOMINAL ORGANS:
Spleen: Normal in size and attenuation, no focal lesions.
Left adrenal gland: Unremarkable.
Right adrenal gland: Absent (prior nephrectomy not documented; if native, cannot be separated from post-surgical changes). Right adrenal gland appears intact and normal.
Kidneys: Normal bilateral renal enhancement and excretion. No hydronephrosis. No renal masses. A 4 mm simple cyst is noted in the lower pole of the right kidney, unchanged.
Stomach: The stomach is decompressed and appears unremarkable. The pylorus-preserving anatomy is noted.
Small and large bowel: The jejunal loops are partially collapsed. The Roux limb reconstruction is identified. No obstruction. No bowel wall thickening or pneumatosis. No free air.

PELVIS:
The urinary bladder is partially decompressed; no filling defects or wall thickening. The uterus and adnexa are not well-evaluated given patient age and hormonal status, but no gross pelvic mass is identified. No inguinal or pelvic lymphadenopathy. No pelvic free fluid.

BONE WINDOWS:
The visualized spine, pelvis, and proximal femora demonstrate no lytic or sclerotic osseous lesions to suggest skeletal metastases. Age-appropriate degenerative changes are present at the lower lumbar spine.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. Significant worsening of hepatic metastatic disease compared with prior imaging. The segment VI hepatic lesion has grown from 0.9 cm to 2.1 cm, and the segment VII lesion has grown from 0.7 cm to 1.6 cm. Two additional new hepatic deposits are now identified in segments IV (1.2 cm) and VIII (0.8 cm), confirming progression with increased tumor burden in the liver. These findings represent clear worsening during therapy with nanoliposomal irinotecan plus 5-FU/leucovorin.

2. No evidence of locoregional recurrence in the Whipple surgical bed. The post-surgical fibrosis and changes are stable in appearance.

3. No pulmonary metastases, no pleural disease, no peritoneal implants, and no evidence of distant osseous involvement on this examination.

4. No new lymphadenopathy.

Correlate with current clinical status and serum CA 19-9 trending. The pattern of hepatic progression with multiple enlarging and new lesions in the setting of second-line chemotherapy is consistent with disease refractory to current treatment.

Dictated and electronically signed by:
Attending Radiologist, Abdominal Imaging Division
